# Regression test for AbstractDeleteEdge plumbing. Deletes the
# cross-aisle segment from (xi=-3, yi=2) to (xi=-2, yi=2) — one
# grid-aligned edge along cross driving aisle 2 between parallel
# aisles 3 and 2. The edge handle is an integer 4-tuple in the
# region's abstract frame, so it stays attached across parameter
# changes.
#
# Note: the stall count is unchanged from baseline here because
# deleting a single cross-aisle segment just splits a face in two
# without removing the area underneath. The test's purpose is to
# verify the code path runs without crashing and survives a
# parameter change; use the -survives-stretch / -survives-rotation
# tests for the "annotation actually affected the output" assertion.

polygon outer
0,0
400,0
400,300
0,300
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

set stalls_per_face=4
----
set stalls_per_face=4

debug use_abstract_stamp=true
----
debug use_abstract_stamp=true

# Baseline (no annotation).
generate
----
total_stalls: 162

annotation abstract-delete-edge region=0x1000000000000 from=-3,2 to=-2,2
----
annotation abstract-delete-edge region=0x0001000000000000 from=-3,2 to=-2,2

generate
----
total_stalls: 162

# Vary aisle_width. row_spacing = 2*effective_depth + 2*aisle_width so
# the parallel-aisle positions slide; the deleted edge in abstract
# coordinates still refers to the same (xi, yi) pair, now at different
# world coordinates.
set aisle_width=14
----
set aisle_width=14

generate
----
total_stalls: 162

screenshot
----
